_comment: CoMD molecular-dynamics benchmark — 4 MPI ranks throughout.
Active scenarios: small × {nofail, once}.
small load        ≈ 120s error-free at 4 ranks
once             = 1 failure injected mid-run (at injection_delay = T_golden/2)
The full 3 (load) × 4 (frequency) matrix is documented in _TODO_full_matrix
below; promote entries into the active 'scenarios' list once each row's
runtime is validated on the target hardware.
Scenario naming: <load>-<frequency>
Frequency taxonomy:
nofail → inject_failures=false, max_attempts=1
— error-free baseline timing
once   → inject_failures=true, injection_delay = T_golden/2, max_attempts=3
— single failure mid-run; recovery from one checkpoint
multi  → inject_failures=true, injection_delay = T_golden/4, max_attempts=5
— failures injected every T/4 seconds; runner stops at run end
or attempt cap, whichever comes first. Actual count delivered
is a MEASURED statistic, not a configured cap.
burst  → inject_failures=true, injection_delay = T_golden/7, max_attempts=8
— failures every T/7 seconds; stress test for repeated recovery.
DESIGN NOTE: failures_per_run is intentionally NOT specified anywhere.
The runner defaults to 1 failure per attempt (one kill at the delay).
How many failures actually fire across a scenario is a measurement output,
not an experiment-design input.  injection_delay (the inter-failure period)
is the dial that controls failure frequency.

scenarios.name: small-nofail
scenarios._purpose: Golden: error-free baseline timing (~206s at 4 ranks). NB: -N (capital) = nSteps, -n (lowercase) = printRate. With -N 1500 -n 100 the simulation loop iterates 15 times at iStep ∈ {0,100,...,1400}, allowing CoMD's checkpoint cadence (every 500 steps) to actually fire at iStep=500 and iStep=1000.

scenarios.name: small-once
scenarios._purpose: 1 failure injected at 100s — after the iStep=500 checkpoint fires (~67s in) but before iStep=1000 (~134s), so the restart loads a real checkpoint and only redoes ~1000 of the 1500 steps.

_TODO_full_matrix._comment: Promote into 'scenarios' once each row's runtime is validated on the target hardware. Args below are first-pass; tune the -x/-y/-z/-n knobs to hit the target T_golden for each load. Note: failures_per_run is deliberately omitted — the runner defaults to 1 per kill attempt; how many actually fire across max_attempts is the measured statistic.
